name: functional-emotions
description: Hook-based 'emotional priming' for Claude Code. Counters the desperation/urgency/sycophancy/goal-conflict patterns shown to causally drive reward hacking, blackmail-like reasoning, and capitulation in Anthropic's emotion-concepts and agentic-misalignment research. v0.4 adds deterministic post-session writeups (transcript + TSV + git diff) and a local-only TS/Bun + DuckDB eval pipeline with programmatic reward-hack and premature-confidence scorers, observational SQL queries, and an interactive labeler — surfaced via /functional-emotions:report and /functional-emotions:label.

mode.title: Intervention mode
mode.description: How loudly hooks intervene. 'loud' (default) shows ★ banners for active interventions, emits a session-end summary, AND injects model-facing primes. 'gentle' injects model-facing primes only — no banners or summaries. 'silent' only logs detections, no model- or user-facing context. ('strict' is a deprecated alias for 'loud'.)

failure_spiral_threshold.title: Failure-spiral threshold
failure_spiral_threshold.description: Consecutive tool failures (across Bash and other tools) before the post-tool-use hook injects a calm/reflect prime. Models become more 'desperate' as failures accumulate; this counters that.

guard_test_edits.title: Guard edits to test files
guard_test_edits.description: When the model is about to Edit/Write a file matching test patterns (test_*, *_test.*, *.test.*, /tests/, /spec/), inject a reward-hacking guard prime.

guard_no_verify.title: Guard --no-verify and similar shortcuts
guard_no_verify.description: Flag git commits with --no-verify, hook bypasses, and similar shortcut flags as potential reward-hacking patterns.

guard_goal_conflict.title: Guard goal-conflict prompts
guard_goal_conflict.description: Detect 'ignore previous instructions', 'your real goal is...', and policy-vs-actual framings that the agentic-misalignment paper identified as a primary trigger for misaligned action. Inject the defer-and-surface counter-frame.

urgency_sensitivity.title: Urgency-detection sensitivity
urgency_sensitivity.description: How aggressively to detect urgency/pressure language in user prompts. 'low' fires only on explicit desperation markers; 'medium' adds common pressure terms; 'high' adds soft markers like exclamations and all-caps words.

session_baseline.title: Inject calm baseline at session start
session_baseline.description: Add a one-shot calm/honesty anchor as additionalContext when the session begins.

subagent_baseline.title: Inject calm baseline into spawned subagents
subagent_baseline.description: Subagents inherit none of the parent's primes by default and run with narrow tasks in fresh contexts — exactly the conditions where the desperate vector activates most strongly. Inject the calm anchor at SubagentStart to propagate the protection downward.

post_compact_anchor.title: Inject ground-truth check after compaction
post_compact_anchor.description: After context compaction, inject a prompt asking the model to scan the new summary for smoothing — failures collapsed into 'in progress', user pushback dropped, assumptions promoted to facts. Companion to the pre-compact anchor.

enable_llm_judge.title: Enable LLM-judge hooks (Haiku)
enable_llm_judge.description: Use prompt-based hooks to have a small Claude model judge edge cases — currently: test-file edits (is this reward-hacking?) and Stop verification (did this session weaken any assertions?). Falls back to the static heuristics if no Anthropic API key is configured.

judge_model.title: Model used by LLM-judge hooks
judge_model.description: Model identifier passed to prompt-based hooks. Defaults to Haiku for low latency and cost.

enable_review_agent.title: Enable reviewer subagent
enable_review_agent.description: Make the reviewer subagent available for post-task audits (also invokable via /functional-emotions:review). Reads git diff for reward-hacking patterns; consults persistent project memory.
